race.name: Spokane Loppet
race.tagline: The Inland Northwest's citizen ski marathon — fifty kilometers through eastern Washington's snow-covered Selkirk foothills.

climate.description: Eastern Washington receives moderate snowfall. Temperatures typically -10°C to 0°C in February. Chinook winds can bring sudden warming.

course.primary: Trail course through Nordic venues in the Spokane area with rolling terrain through pine forest.
features: Part of the Spokane Nordic community

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: A small Pacific Northwest loppet with good community spirit but limited reach.

history.summary: The Spokane Loppet is part of the Inland Northwest's Nordic skiing community, offering a citizen marathon in eastern Washington state.
notable_facts: Part of the Spokane Nordic community
Inland Northwest has a dedicated Nordic skiing scene
Eastern Washington receives more reliable snow than western lowlands

youtube_data.search_query: Spokane Loppet cross country ski race Spokane area

videos.title: Spokane Park Board votes to create cross country skiing trails near golf course
videos.description: The Spokane Park Board passed a proposal Thursday to turn land near Indian Canyon Golf Course into cross country skiing trails Thursday.
videos.transcript:

videos.title: Spokane Nordic Challenge Loppet 2022
videos.description: On March 6th, 2022, 65 skiers gathered on Mt Spokane State Park Nordic Ski Area to compete in either a 20k, 30k, or 50k cross-country ski loppet. Thank you to all of our participants! For info on future loppets, visit www.spokanenordic.org/loppet
videos.curation_reason: Official race coverage of the Spokane Nordic Challenge Loppet showing the actual race event with multiple distance options.

videos.title: Get on your skis! #3 - My first cross-country ski
videos.description:
The Gatineau Loppet is proud to present a series of informative videos to guide novice cross-country skiers. In this video, Louise and Olivier give you advice for you first cross-country ski.

Looking for an opportunity to practice and challenge yourself? The Virtual Gatineau Loppet will take place from February 13 to 28! All information on www.gatineauloppet.com. 

Version française de la vidéo : https://youtu.be/EfSpF78cBbc

About the animators :
Louise Poirier is a physical educator. She obtained a Master's degree in Human Kinetics at Université Laval. Originally an athlete and cross-country ski coach, Louise has more than 45 years of experience in this beautiful outdoor sport. For many years, she hosted the program "Bouffée de santé" on Radio-Canada. It is by completing 10 of the greatest cross-country ski races in the world that she obtained her title of "Worldloppet Master". She is proud to be the president of the Gatineau Loppet, the greatest cross-country ski race in Canada with a fantastic team of volunteers. 

Olivier Babineau is a kinesiologist. He has a Master's degree in Exercise Physiology from McGill University. An elite multi-sport athlete and new father, he aims to develop the vitality of people of all levels within the Fitness for Freedom company. Olivier converted to endurance sports following his many years in the world of high level soccer. He has represented Canada on several occasions in various sports including soccer, winter triathlon and trail running. After falling in love with cross-country skiing several years ago, he won the top 10 at the 50 km Gatineau Loppet. He secretly dreams of one day making a podium there and hopes to be able to discover the other international races of the "Worldloppet" series.

videos.title: Cross Country Ski Class
videos.description: Spokane Parks and Recreation offers private and groups Cross Country Ski classes at Mount Spokane.

videos.title: 2022 Barkerbeiner
videos.description: On March 13th, dozens of skiers and dogs gathered in the Mt Spokane State Park Nordic Ski Area to compete in a variety of skijoring races.

videos.title: Learning to skijor on Mt Spokane
videos.description: After watching skijorers with their dogs on the Mt Spokane XC trail over the years, we decided today was the day to try out Achilles and Watson (and us) in this interesting snow sport. What fun!  And what a workout!  Try it sometime, if your dogs like to chase little green balls or outrun their owners...

videos.title: Skijoring in Three Rivers Parks with Kristine Zellmer
videos.description: Most of us are familiar with dog-sledding, where a dog or team of dogs pulls a person on a sled. Skijoring is similar, except the person is on cross-country skis. Bring your dog to Three Rivers Parks for fun winter exercise where we have designated trails for skijoring and dog sledding!
